MeasurementToolMetadata.Description: Revised Social Anhedonia Scale - short form (RSAS, 15 items)
MeasurementToolMetadata.Instructions: For each of the following items, select either True or False.

soc_anhed_1.Description: Having close friends is not as important as many people say.

soc_anhed_2.Description: I never had really close friends in high school.

soc_anhed_3.Description: I prefer watching television to going out with other people.

soc_anhed_4.Description: Just being with friends can make me feel really good.

soc_anhed_5.Description: I'm much too independent to really get involved with other people.

soc_anhed_6.Description: I prefer hobbies and leisure activities that do not involve other people.

soc_anhed_7.Description: I don't really feel very close to my friends.

soc_anhed_8.Description: People who try to get to know me better usually give up after awhile.

soc_anhed_9.Description: Knowing that I have friends who care about me gives me a sense of security.

soc_anhed_10.Description: People are usually better off if they stay aloof from emotional involvements with most others.

soc_anhed_11.Description: If given the choice, I would much rather be with others than be alone.

soc_anhed_12.Description: Although there are things that I enjoy doing by myself, I usually seem to have more fun when I do things with other people.

soc_anhed_13.Description: I feel pleased and gratified as I learn more and more about the emotional life of my friends.

soc_anhed_14.Description: When things are going really good for my close friends, it makes me feel good too.

soc_anhed_15.Description: Making new friends isn't worth the energy it takes.

rsasShort_score_total.Description: Social anhedonia total (RSAS short): items 1,2,3,5,6,7,8,10,15 scored as-is (True=1) plus items 4,9,11,12,13,14 reverse-coded (1-x), then summed. Range 0-15. n/a if any item missing.
